seoTitle: How to Verify Your Domain in Brevo
metaDescription: Authenticate your sending domain in Brevo with DKIM and DMARC records to protect deliverability and meet Gmail, Yahoo, and Microsoft sender requirements.
intro: Sender reputation and inbox deliverability depend heavily on domain authentication, and Brevo requires this to meet modern Gmail, Yahoo, and Microsoft compliance standards. Verifying your domain involves adding a few DNS records, either through Brevo's automatic sync with your provider or by mapping them manually.

steps.heading: Open the senders workspace
steps.body: In your Brevo account, navigate to the senders and domains settings to begin the authentication process.

steps.heading: Add your sending domain
steps.body: Enter your professional custom domain without a browser prefix or folder path, since raw prefixes can cause verification errors.

steps.heading: Choose an automatic or manual verification path
steps.body: Depending on your DNS provider, Brevo may offer an automated handshake to inject the necessary records, or you can add the Brevo code, DKIM, and DMARC records manually.

steps.heading: Add the required DNS records
steps.body: Copy the standard text records Brevo provides into your domain provider's DNS settings if you're not using the automatic sync option.

steps.heading: Check propagation and confirm verification
steps.body: DNS changes can take time to propagate, so check back until Brevo confirms your domain shows as verified and locked in.

faq.question: Why do I need to verify my domain in Brevo?
faq.answer: Domain verification protects your sender reputation and is required to meet current Gmail, Yahoo, and Microsoft inbox compliance standards.

faq.question: What DNS records does domain verification require?
faq.answer: Typically a Brevo verification code along with DKIM and DMARC records need to be added to your domain's DNS settings.

faq.question: How long does DNS verification take?
faq.answer: It depends on your DNS provider's propagation time, which can range from minutes to up to 48 hours in some cases.

faq.question: What happens if I don't verify my domain?
faq.answer: Unverified domains risk higher bounce rates and are more likely to be filtered by major email providers' security systems.
